fields.label: alias
fields.tooltip: Used to make any URLs used in the test unique to the user (for example, it affects the redirect url), set to something unique to avoid clashes with other testers, e.g. set to your company name. Do not include any spaces or non-URL safe characters. If using dynamic client registration, this can be left blank and a random one will be generated. For some ecosystems (e.g. KSA) regulated entities should use the alias value assigned to them.

fields.label: description
fields.tooltip: Freeform text to help you identify this test run (optional). This will be publicly visible when your certification is published.

fields.label: publish
fields.tooltip: Whether test results should be made visible to other users. "Everything" will reveal configuration information, including secret keys. Use "summary" if these details should remain private while the overall results are public. If in doubt choose "No" - test results can be made public later.

fields.label: Transmitter Issuer
fields.tooltip: Transmitter's issuer URL.

fields.label: Transmitter Configuration URL
fields.tooltip: Transmitter's Configuration URL.

fields.label: Transmitter Metadata Suffix
fields.tooltip: Suffix to append to the ssf-configuration URL.

fields.label: Transmitter access_token
fields.tooltip: Access token to use in SSF request. This may be left empty, in which case the request will not contain an access token.

fields.label: Push Endpoint URL
fields.tooltip: Receiver endpoint URL to push events to on PUSH delivery mode.

fields.label: SSF Stream Audience
fields.tooltip: Audience to use for the SSF Stream.

fields.label: SSF valid SubjectId
fields.tooltip: Existing RFC 9493 SubjectId to generate SSF events for. The SubjectId must be valid according to RFC 9493 Subject Identifiers for Security Event Tokens. For example, {"format":"email", "email":"foo@example.com"}

fields.label: SSF invalid SubjectId
fields.tooltip: Non existing RFC 9493 SubjectId to generate events. The SubjectId must be valid according to RFC 9493 Subject Identifiers for Security Event Tokens.

fields.label: Credential Issuer URL
fields.tooltip: Credential Issuer URL.

fields.label: Signing JWK
fields.tooltip: Signing key to be used for issuing credentials, in JWK format. Note that the JWK must also contain the signing certificate chain in the x5c claim.

fields.label: Credential Trust Anchor
fields.tooltip: The trust anchor certificate for verifying the SD-JWT credential x5c chain, as a PEM certificate.

fields.label: Status List Trust Anchor
fields.tooltip: The trust anchor certificate for verifying the status list token x5c chain, as a PEM certificate.

fields.label: Credential Configuration ID
fields.tooltip: The credential_configuration_id to use. Defaults to eu.europa.ec.eudi.pid.1.

fields.label: Credential Proof Type Hint
fields.tooltip: The credential proof type hint to use. If empty we'll use the first proof type that's listed in the referenced credential configuration.

fields.label: Credential Issuer Metadata URL
fields.tooltip: Full URL that server will use for OID4VCI credential issuer metadata.

fields.label: Credential Offer Endpoint URL
fields.tooltip: Full URL for the OID4VCI credential offer endpoint. If you are using HAIP, use 'haip-vci://'

fields.label: Authorization Server URL Override
fields.tooltip: Full issuer URL for the OAuth2 Authorization Server. When this is not set, the testsuite will use the discoveryIssuer as authorizationServer, otherwise when this is set the testsuite will use this authorization server to obtain an access_token for the tests.

fields.label: Static tx_code
fields.tooltip: Static tx_code value for pre-authorized code flow. Useful for CI integrations. Leave empty if the tx_value is supplied during test execution. The test will expose a /tx_code endpoint that can be used to supply the tx_code via GET $BASE_URL/tx_code?code=your_tx_code.

fields.label: issuer
fields.tooltip: Full URL that server will use as the 'iss' value in id_token.

fields.label: jwks_uri
fields.tooltip: Full URL to server's JWKS endpoint.

fields.label: authorization_endpoint
fields.tooltip: Full URL to server's authorization endpoint. When testing a wallet use 'openid4vp://' or similar.

fields.label: token_endpoint
fields.tooltip: Full URL to server's token endpoint.

fields.label: userinfo_endpoint
fields.tooltip: Full URL to server's userinfo endpoint.

fields.label: acr_values
fields.tooltip: Space separated list of server's supported acr values that will be sent in the acr_values field; if left blank '1 2' is the default.

fields.label: discoveryUrl
fields.tooltip: Full URL to server's .well-known/openid-configuration.

fields.label: jwks
fields.tooltip: Private key to be used by the server to sign id_tokens, this should be one key in JSON Web Key Set format.

fields.label: login_hint
fields.tooltip: A value that the server accepts in the login_hint parameter. If not specified a default value ('buffy@<your issuer></cts-icon>') will be used.

fields.label: ui_locales
fields.tooltip: A value that the server accepts in the ui_locales parameter. If not specified a default value ('se') will be used.

fields.label: PDP Identifier
fields.tooltip: Polic Decision Point Identifier

fields.label: Access Evaluation API Endpoint
fields.tooltip: Access Evaluation API Endpoint

fields.label: Access Evaluations API Endpoint
fields.tooltip: Access Evaluations API Endpoint

fields.label: Subject Entity Search API Endpoint
fields.tooltip: Subject Entity Search API Endpoint

fields.label: Action Entity Search API Endpoint
fields.tooltip: Action Entity Search API Endpoint

fields.label: Resource Entity Search API Endpoint
fields.tooltip: Resource Entity Search API Endpoint

fields.label: PDP API Key
fields.tooltip: API Endpoint Key

fields.label: client_id
fields.tooltip: OAuth client id used with the authorization server. For OID4VP tests this can be left blank if using x509_san_dns or redirect_uri client id schemes.

fields.label: client_secret
fields.tooltip: Client secret.

fields.label: client_name
fields.tooltip: Client name to use in dynamic registration request. This may be left empty, in which case a random name is used.

fields.label: initial_access_token
fields.tooltip: Access token to use in dynamic registration request. This may be left empty, in which case the request will not contain an access token.

fields.label: scope
fields.tooltip: Scopes to use in authorization request, e.g. 'openid accounts'. For Brazil, you may use the 'accounts' or the 'payments' scope depending which API endpoint you want to use for testing.

fields.label: authorization_encrypted_response_alg
fields.tooltip: JWE alg algorithm for encrypting the JARM response issued to this Client, e.g RSA-OAEP or ECDH-ESP. For VP tests this can be left empty and the suite will use a default.

fields.label: authorization_encrypted_response_enc
fields.tooltip: JWE enc algorithm for encrypting the JARM response issued to this Client, e.g. A128CBC-HS256 or A256GCM. For VP tests this can be left empty and the suite will use a default.

fields.label: presentation_definition
fields.tooltip: presentation_definition to use in authorization request

fields.label: dcql
fields.tooltip: The DCQL (Digital Credentials Query Language) to use in dcql_query parameter in authorization request

fields.label: redirect_uri
fields.tooltip: URI the conformance suite should redirect back to with the result of the authorization endpoint call appended.

fields.label: backchannel_client_notification_endpoint
fields.tooltip: This is the endpoint to which the conformance suite will post a notification after a successful or failed end-user authentication.

fields.label: jwks
fields.tooltip: Client's private signing key in JSON Web Key Set format, must begin with { "keys": [ { PEM/DER format private keys MUST be correctly converted to a JWKS, e.g. using 'pem-jwk'. The JWKS should normally contain exactly one JWK, which will be used for client assertions / signing requests, and must contain an 'alg' field indicating what algorithm to use. If the OP issues encrypted id tokens a key with 'use': 'enc' must be provided, which must be the second entry in the JWKS.

fields.label: organization jwks
fields.tooltip: Organizations's BRSEAL private signing key in JSON Web Key Set format, to be used to sign API requests to the payment consent creation/payment initiation endpoints. This key must be for the organization listed in the client's TLS certificate subjectdn. Only required if 'scope' contains 'payments'.

fields.label: client_secret_jwt_alg
fields.tooltip: Algorithm for client_secret_jwt authentication, defaults to HS256.

fields.label: id_token_encrypted_response_alg
fields.tooltip: JWE alg algorithm for encrypting the ID Token issued to this Client, e.g RSA-OAEP.

fields.label: id_token_encrypted_response_enc
fields.tooltip: JWE enc algorithm for encrypting the ID Token issued to this Client, defaults to A128CBC-HS256.

fields.label: certificate
fields.tooltip: TLS client certificate in base64-encoded DER (in JSON string, no headers) or OpenSSL/PEM format - often has .pem file extension.

fields.label: hint_type
fields.tooltip: Type of hint to identify user. For Open Finance Brazil, only login_hint and id_token_hint are allowed.

fields.label: hint_value
fields.tooltip: Hint value to identify user, e.g. a login_hint, login_hint_token or id_token_hint.

fields.label: post_logout_redirect_uri
fields.tooltip: URI the conformance suite should redirect back to after the end_session_endpoint call.

fields.label: frontchannel_logout_uri
fields.tooltip: Front channel logout URI.

fields.label: backchannel_logout_uri
fields.tooltip: Back channel logout URI.

fields.label: DPoP Signing Alg
fields.tooltip: JWS algorithm for signing DPoP proofs

sections.title: tls_client_auth configuration (Enter only one of the following)

fields.label: tls_client_auth_subject_dn
fields.tooltip: Certificate subject DN, e.g cn=certification.example.com,o=oidf,st=ca,c=us.

fields.label: tls_client_auth_san_dns
fields.tooltip: DNSname subject alternative name, e.g client.example.com.

fields.label: tls_client_auth_san_uri
fields.tooltip: URI subject alternative name, e.g https://example.com.

fields.label: tls_client_auth_san_ip
fields.tooltip: IP address subject alternative name, e.g 10.0.0.1 or 2001:0db8:85a3:0000:0000:8a2e:0370:7334.

fields.label: tls_client_auth_san_email
fields.tooltip: Email address subject alternative name, e.g john@example.com.

sections.title: TLS certificates for client (used to make MTLS connections)

fields.label: mtls.cert
fields.tooltip: TLS client certificate in base64-encoded DER (in JSON string, no headers) or OpenSSL/PEM format - often has .pem file extension.

fields.label: mtls.key
fields.tooltip: Unencrypted TLS client key in base64-encoded DER or PEM format (in JSON string no headers) or OpenSSL/PEM format.

fields.label: mtls.ca
fields.tooltip: TLS client CA in base64-encoded DER (in JSON string, no headers) or OpenSSL/PEM format. Optional, can be omitted if using self-signed certificates or if the server does not require any certificate authority elements to be supplied.

fields.label: client_id
fields.tooltip: OAuth client id used with the authorization server for a client that supports client_secret_post. If the server allows a single client to use both client_secret_basic and client_secret_post then this can be the same client as above.

fields.label: client_secret
fields.tooltip: Client secret for a client that supports client_secret_post. If the server allows a single client to use both client_secret_basic and client_secret_post then this can be the same client as above.

fields.label: client_id
fields.tooltip: OAuth client id used with the authorization server.

fields.label: client_secret
fields.tooltip: Client secret

fields.label: client_name
fields.tooltip: Second client name to use in dynamic registration request. This may be left empty, in which case a random name is used.

fields.label: initial_access_token
fields.tooltip: Access token to use in dynamic registration request. This may be left empty, in which case the request will not contain an access token.

fields.label: scope
fields.tooltip: Scopes to use in authorization request, e.g. 'openid accounts'.

fields.label: redirect_uri
fields.tooltip: URI the conformance suite should redirect back to with the result of the authorization endpoint call appended.

fields.label: jwks
fields.tooltip: Second client's private signing key in JSON Web Key Set format, must begin with { "keys": [ { PEM/DER format private keys MUST be correctly converted to a JWKS, e.g. using 'pem-jwk'. The JWKS should normally contain exactly one JWK, which will be used for client assertions / signing requests, and must contain an 'alg' field indicating what algorithm to use. If the OP issues encrypted id tokens a key with 'use': 'enc' must be provided, which must be the second entry in the JWKS. The key must be different to that for the first client.

fields.label: organization jwks
fields.tooltip: Second client's organization's BRSEAL private signing key in JSON Web Key Set format, to be used to sign API requests to the payment consent creation/payment initiation endpoints. This key must be for the organization listed in the client's TLS certificate subjectdn. Both clients can be from the same organization, in which case the same organization private key should be provided for both clients. Only required if 'scope' contains 'payments'.

fields.label: certificate
fields.tooltip: TLS client certificate in base64-encoded DER (in JSON string, no headers) or OpenSSL/PEM format - often has .pem file extension.

fields.label: client_secret_jwt_alg
fields.tooltip: Algorithm for client_secret_jwt authentication, defaults to HS256.

fields.label: id_token_encrypted_response_alg
fields.tooltip: JWE alg algorithm for encrypting the ID Token issued to this Client, e.g RSA-OAEP.

fields.label: id_token_encrypted_response_enc
fields.tooltip: JWE enc algorithm for encrypting the ID Token issued to this Client, defaults to A128CBC-HS256.

fields.label: acr_value
fields.tooltip: If this server supports acr, this should contain a valid acr value that can be requested in an authentication request, e.g. 'urn:openbanking:psd2:sca'.

fields.label: DPoP Signing Alg
fields.tooltip: JWS algorithm for signing DPoP proofs

fields.label: mtls2.cert
fields.tooltip: TLS client certificate in base64-encoded DER (in JSON string, no headers) or OpenSSL/PEM format.

fields.label: mtls2.key
fields.tooltip: Unencrypted TLS client key in base64-encoded DER or PEM format (in JSON string no headers) or OpenSSL/PEM format.

fields.label: mtls2.ca
fields.tooltip: TLS client CA in base64-encoded DER (in JSON string, no headers) or OpenSSL/PEM format.

fields.label: testHost
fields.tooltip: Hostname for TLS connection tests (only used in sample-test-test).

fields.label: testPort
fields.tooltip: Port number for TLS connection tests (only used in sample-test-test).

fields.label: resourceUrl
fields.tooltip: URL to use in resource server tests, must return a JSON response. For UK OpenBanking tests, this should be the base url for UK AISP APIs and hence must end '/open-banking/vX.Y/aisp/' (this form of URL is required by the UK OpenBanking specs). For Brazil OpenBanking you should use the accounts endpoint url, or the payments endpoint if you specify payments in the scope. For Brazil OpenInsurance, use the resources endpoint url. For KSA OpenBanking, use the accounts endpoint url.

fields.label: resourceMethod
fields.tooltip: The HTTP method to be used at the resource endpoint.

fields.label: resourceMediaType
fields.tooltip: The ContentType of the data being sent to the resource endpoint in a POST request. eg. 'application/json'

fields.label: resourceRequestBody
fields.tooltip: The data. in JSON format, to be send in a POST request to the resource endpoint

fields.label: consentUrl
fields.tooltip: URL for the consent endpoint. This should be the data read consent endpoint. If you are testing OpenBanking payments, it should be the payment initiation consent endpoint and you should include 'payments' in the scope field.

fields.label: resourceUrlAccountRequests
fields.tooltip: Base URL to use in resource account request server tests. For UK OpenBanking: optional, use if endpoints split across servers. For SAMA/KSA OpenBanking: the full url for the account-access-consents endpoint.

fields.label: resourceUrlAccountsResource
fields.tooltip: Base URL to use in resource accounts server tests (optional, use if endpoints split across servers).

fields.label: cdrVersion
fields.tooltip: The version number to send in the x-v HTTP header to the resource endpoint.

fields.label: brazilCpf
fields.tooltip: The 'CPF' value to be used in the consent creation request. At least one of CPF or CNPJ must be supplied.

fields.label: brazilCnpj
fields.tooltip: The 'CNPJ' value to be used in the consent creation request. At least one of CPF or CNPJ must be supplied.

fields.label: Business or Personal Products
fields.tooltip: For accounts/resources tests, whether test should be run for Business or Personal Products.

fields.label: Resource server organization id
fields.tooltip: The organization id for the resource server, as recorded in the directory. Only required if 'scope' contains 'payments'.

fields.label: Payment consent request JSON
fields.tooltip: JSON representation of a payment consent request. This will be used as the body of the request sent to the payment consent endpoint. Only required if 'scope' contains 'payments'.

fields.label: Payment initiation request JSON
fields.tooltip: JSON representation of a PIX payment request. This will be used as the body of the request sent to the payment initiation endpoint. Only required if 'scope' contains 'payments'.

fields.label: institution_id
fields.tooltip: Financial institution ID to send to FAPI resources, i.e. Institution ID assigned by UK OpenBanking. This can be left empty if the resource server does not require this header.

fields.label: Authorization Request JSON
fields.tooltip: JSON representation of the authorization. This will be used as the authorization_request parameter of the authorization.

fields.label: Supported Authorization Request Types
fields.tooltip: JSON representation of the list of authorization types. This will be used to validate RAR payloads submitted by RPs.

fields.label: Discovery Endpoint
fields.tooltip: URL to the OpenBanking Directory. e.g. for Brazil directory sandbox use https://auth.sandbox.directory.openbankingbrasil.org.br/.well-known/openid-configuration

fields.label: Directory ClientID
fields.tooltip: client_id for this client on the OpenBanking Directory. This is used to obtain an access token, which is used to retrieve the software statement. The MTLS certificate provided for the client MUST be one that can be used to access the OpenBanking directory. An example client/configuration is linked to from the instructions (see link at top of this page).

fields.label: Directory API base
fields.tooltip: Base URL for the OpenBanking Directory. e.g. for Brazil Directory sandbox use https://matls-api.sandbox.directory.openbankingbrasil.org.br/

fields.label: Directory keystore base
fields.tooltip: Keystore URL for the OpenBanking Directory. e.g. for Brazil Directory sandbox use https://keystore.sandbox.directory.openbankingbrasil.org.br/

fields.label: eKYC Request Unverified Claims
fields.tooltip: List of unverified claims to request in each test. Must be a single claim name or a JSON array of claim names.

fields.label: eKYC Request Verified Claims
fields.tooltip: List of verified claims to request in each test. Must be a single claim name or a JSON array of claim names.

fields.label: eKYC Verified Claims Request
fields.tooltip: Custom claims request. Must be a valid json object that can be added directly to the request as the top level 'claims' element. Must be a single JSON object or a JSON array of objects.

fields.label: eKYC UserInfo
fields.tooltip: A complete set of end-user data as held by the OpenID Provider in the form it would be returned via the userinfo endpoint. Enter the entire userinfo json. Must be a single JSON object or a JSON array of objects.

fields.label: eKYC Expected Response
fields.tooltip: JSON obect containing expected verified_claims values for end-user data keyed by the sub identifier in the ID Token or UserInfo responses. Response claim values will be compared to this list when no explicit value is requested.

fields.label: eKYC Additional Request Validation Schemas
fields.tooltip: Additional schemas to validate eKYC requests. Must be a single JSON object or a JSON array of object, each of which is a valid JSON schema object.

fields.label: eKYC Additional Response Validation Schemas
fields.tooltip: Additional schemas to validate eKYC responses. Must be a single JSON object or a JSON array of object, each of which is a valid JSON schema object

sections.title: Federation entity under test
sections.intro: This section relates to the entity under test, i.e. your federation entity, the entity that will be the primary subject of the test suite. See also the detailed instructions.

fields.label: entity_identifier
fields.tooltip: The entity identifier of the federation entity that the test will interface with. This URL MUST use the https scheme, have a host component, and MAY contain port and path components. It MUST NOT contain query parameter or fragment components.

fields.label: trust_anchor
fields.tooltip: The trust anchor of the federation entity under test

fields.label: trust_anchor_jwks
fields.tooltip: Pre-configured keys for the entity's trust anchor.

sections.title: Test suite OP
sections.intro: This section relates to the test suite and how it behaves as an OpenID provider federation entity. See also the detailed instructions.

fields.label: op_ec_jwks
fields.tooltip: JWKSet containing the private key for signing the entity configuration published by the test suite entity

fields.label: op_server_jwks
fields.tooltip: JWKSet containing the private keys for signing and encryption, used by the tests suite to sign id_tokens and decrypt request objects (if applicable) when acting as an OP.

fields.label: op_entity_identifier_override
fields.tooltip: When set, overrides the default entity identifier for the test OP otherwise created by the test suite. The test suite must be reachable using the specified host, i.e. if the override is 'https://proxy.example.com' then that host must proxy the requests to the actual hostname of the test.

sections.title: Test suite RP
sections.intro: This section relates to the test suite and how it behaves as an OpenID relying party federation entity. See also the detailed instructions.

fields.label: rp_ec_jwks
fields.tooltip: JWKSet containing the private key for signing the entity configuration published by the test suite entity

fields.label: rp_client_jwks
fields.tooltip: JWKSet containing the private key for signing, used by the tests suite to sign request objects.

fields.label: rp_entity_identifier_override
fields.tooltip: When set, overrides the default entity identifier for the test OP otherwise created by the test suite. The test suite must be reachable using the specified host, i.e. if the override is 'https://proxy.example.com' then that host must proxy the requests to the actual hostname of the test.

sections.title: Test suite trust anchor
sections.intro: This section relates to the test suite and how it behaves as a trust anchor. See also the detailed instructions.

fields.label: immediate_subordinates
fields.tooltip: The trust anchor's immediate subordinates, i.e. the federation entities that will appear in the list response. Each row in the text area corresponds to an element in the list response array. Each row needs to contain a valid entity identifier. The test suite's entity identifier is added automatically.

fields.label: trust_anchor_jwks
fields.tooltip: The JWKSet containing the private signing key that the test suite trust anchor will use to sign entity statements.

fields.label: Client Attester Keys JWKS
fields.tooltip: Pre-configured keys of the client attester in JWKS format. Note the Client Attester Certificate must be provided in the x5c JWK claim as a JSON array with the encoded certificate

fields.label: Client Attestation Issuer
fields.tooltip: URI used as the client attestation issuer

fields.label: Client Attestation Trust Anchor
fields.tooltip: The trust anchor of the Client Attestation as PEM certificate

fields.label: Key Attestation JWKS
fields.placeholder: REQUIRED if Key Attestation is used
fields.tooltip: Pre-configured keys of the key attestation JWKS format. Leave empty if Key attestation is not used. Note the Client Attester Certificate must be provided in the x5c JWK claim as a JSON array with the encoded certificate.

fields.label: Key Attestation Trust Anchor
fields.placeholder: REQUIRED if Key Attestation is used
fields.tooltip: The trust anchor of the Key Attestation as PEM certificate. Leave empty if Key attestation is not used.
